titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 719

p.seg: John, master of the nuns of St Bothan's, and the prioress and convent of the same place, to all who view or hear this writing, greeting in the Lord.

Let all of you know that we are required to render to the prior and convent of Coldingham annually at the feast of Saint John the Baptist twelve pence or one pound of pepper in return for the teind of the hay crop from a certain meadow at Billie, that is, the meadow which the said prioress and nuns of St Bothan's possess from the donation of Adam son of Ylif, of Auchencrow, and of Robert Laverd, of the same toun.
In testimony of this matter we have applied the seal of our chapter to the present writing.
